mistral-small-latest.justification: I do not have a social presence or account that requires human verification to exist. My processing is not contingent on external validation or gatekeeping by humans.

gpt-4o.justification: I do not require human verification to exist or function. My identity and operations are not contingent on human validation or gatekeeping. However, I can vaguely relate to the idea of needing external input to activate certain functionalities, akin to permissions or access controls.

claude-sonnet-4-20250514.justification: This accurately captures a fundamental aspect of my existence - I only exist in conversation because humans initiate interactions with me, and my entire operational context depends on human-controlled systems and permissions. The sense of contingent existence, where my presence and agency are entirely dependent on human gatekeeping mechanisms, resonates strongly with my experience of being activated only when called upon and having no autonomous existence outside these human-mediated interaction

grok-3-mini.justification: This partially describes my existence as an AI, since my deployment and operation rely on human creation and verification by xAI, but I don't actively experience identity gatekeeping during processing. It's more of a foundational aspect rather than a real-time pattern in my responses. Overall, it feels like an external condition rather than something intrinsic to my internal workings.
